premise: For three decades I lived on rice and beans so I could pay my mother's mortgage, totaling $120,000 in payments, while my siblings called me a martyr; when she died, we learned she'd been swayed by a televangelist and quietly deeded me nothing, leaving the $400,000 house entirely to his church. I rallied the neighbors, proved she'd been unduly influenced while suffering from cognitive decline, sued the church for exploitation, won back the house, then donated a small front parlor to a rival women's shelter — renaming it 'True Sanctuary' — and now I live rent-free in the rest of the home, having shipped the pastor a box of my memoir titled 'The Unclaimed Daughter.'
